_meta.scope_note: Hash, set, sorted-set and stream commands. All entries verified against Redis 8.2.1 via tools/resp_inspect.py. Examples reflect real observed replies.
_meta.universal_errors: Per spec §6, errors emitted uniformly across the family are documented here rather than per command: `WRONGTYPE Operation against a key holding the wrong kind of value` (type-specific commands when the key holds an incompatible value), `ERR wrong number of arguments for '<cmd>' command` (arity mismatch on any command), and module-not-loaded / transient operational errors (OOM, LOADING, MASTERDOWN, etc.). Command-specific errors — `hash value is not an integer` for HINCRBY, `INCR option supports a single increment-element pair` for ZADD, `Invalid stream ID specified as stream command argument` for XADD/XRANGE, etc. — ARE enumerated per command.

returns.summary: Number of fields newly added to the hash (existing fields updated in place do not count).

resp2.summary: Count of fields newly created. Updates to existing fields are not counted.

returns.summary: Value of the field, or null if the field or key does not exist.

oneof.when: field or key does not exist

returns.summary: Values for the requested fields, in the same order, with null in the position of any field not present in the hash.

resp2.summary: Values for the requested fields, in the requested order. Missing fields are returned as null in their position.

items.summary: Field value, or null if the field is not in the hash. A missing key behaves the same as a hash with no matching fields (every position is null).

returns.summary: All field-value pairs from the hash at the key, in arbitrary order. Empty if the key does not exist.

resp2.summary: Flat array of alternating field names and values, in arbitrary order. Empty array if the key does not exist.

resp3.summary: Map of field names to values; map keys are unordered. Empty map if the key does not exist.

returns.summary: Whether the field exists in the hash.

resp2.summary: 1 if the field exists, 0 if it does not exist or the key is missing.

returns.summary: Field names of the hash, in arbitrary order. Empty if the key does not exist.

resp2.summary: Field names of the hash, in arbitrary order. Empty array if the key does not exist.

returns.summary: Field values of the hash, in arbitrary order. Empty if the key does not exist.

resp2.summary: Field values of the hash, in arbitrary order. Empty array if the key does not exist.

returns.summary: Number of fields in the hash.

resp2.summary: Number of fields in the hash, or 0 if the key does not exist.

returns.summary: Number of fields actually removed (fields named but not present do not count).

resp2.summary: Number of fields removed from the hash. Named-but-absent fields and missing keys both contribute 0.

returns.summary: String length of the field's value, or 0 if the field or key does not exist.

resp2.summary: Length of the string stored at the field, or 0 if the field or key is missing. Note: returns 0 (not null) for absent fields.

returns.summary: Value of the field after the increment, or an error when the field's current value isn't an integer or the result would overflow.

oneof.summary: Field value after the increment. The field is created with value 0 before incrementing if it did not exist.

oneof.when: field's stored value isn't an integer

messages.value: hash value is not an integer
messages.summary: The field exists but its value can't be parsed as an integer.

oneof.when: result would overflow signed 64-bit integer range

messages.summary: The increment would push the field's value outside the signed 64-bit range.

returns.summary: Number of members newly added to the set (members already present are not counted).

resp2.summary: Count of members newly added. Members that were already in the set are not counted.

returns.summary: Number of members in the set.

resp2.summary: Cardinality (number of members) of the set, or 0 if the key does not exist.

returns.summary: Whether the value is a member of the set.

resp2.summary: 1 if the value is a member of the set, 0 if it is not or the key does not exist.

returns.summary: All members of the set, in arbitrary order. Empty if the key does not exist.

resp2.summary: All members of the set, in arbitrary order. Empty array if the key does not exist.

resp3.summary: All members of the set; sets are unordered by definition. Empty set if the key does not exist.

examples.label: key exists
examples.note: RESP3 set is rendered here as a JSON array (per §3.1). Set semantics — unordered, unique — apply.

returns.summary: Number of members actually removed (members named but not present do not count).

resp2.summary: Number of members removed from the set. Members not present and missing keys both contribute 0.

returns.summary: Number of new members added (default), the updated score (with INCR), null when an XX/NX/LT/GT condition aborts the operation, or an error for incompatible option combinations or non-numeric scores.

oneof.summary: Operation aborted by a conflict with one of the XX/NX/LT/GT options.
Number of members newly added (not counting score updates).
Number of members newly added plus number whose score was changed.
Updated score of the member, as a string.

oneof.when: INCR is given with more than one member

messages.value: INCR option supports a single increment-element pair

oneof.when: XX and NX are both specified

messages.value: XX and NX options at the same time are not compatible

oneof.when: GT, LT, or NX are combined in an unsupported way

messages.value: GT, LT, and/or NX options at the same time are not compatible

oneof.when: a score argument is not a valid float

messages.value: value is not a valid float

oneof.summary: Number of members newly added (not counting score updates).
Number of members newly added plus number whose score was changed.

oneof.when: INCR is given with more than one member

messages.value: INCR option supports a single increment-element pair

oneof.when: XX and NX are both specified

messages.value: XX and NX options at the same time are not compatible

oneof.when: GT, LT, or NX are combined in an unsupported way

messages.value: GT, LT, and/or NX options at the same time are not compatible

oneof.when: a score argument is not a valid float

messages.value: value is not a valid float

returns.summary: Number of members in the sorted set.

resp2.summary: Cardinality (number of members) of the sorted set, or 0 if the key does not exist.

returns.summary: Score of the member, or null if the member or key is missing.

oneof.summary: Score of the member (double-precision floating point), as a string.

returns.summary: Zero-based position of the member in ascending-score order, or null if the member or key is missing.

oneof.summary: Zero-based rank of the member in ascending-score order.

returns.summary: Members in the specified range, in ascending score order. With WITHSCORES, each member is paired with its score, but RESP2 and RESP3 carry the pairing differently.

oneof.summary: Members in ascending score order; ties broken lexicographically.
Flat array of alternating member and score, in ascending score order. Scores are bulk strings encoding doubles.
Members in ascending score order; ties broken lexicographically.
Array of [member, score] 2-tuples, in ascending score order.

examples.label: WITHSCORES
examples.note: Structural divergence: RESP2 flattens member/score pairs into a single array; RESP3 emits each pair as a 2-tuple.

returns.summary: ID of the added entry, null with NOMKSTREAM when the key does not exist, or an error when the supplied ID is malformed or not strictly greater than the stream's current top ID.

oneof.summary: ID of the added entry, in `<ms>-<seq>` form. Auto-generated when `*` is supplied; otherwise the user-specified ID is returned.

oneof.when: NOMKSTREAM set and key does not exist
supplied ID is not greater than the stream's current top ID

messages.value: The ID specified in XADD is equal or smaller than the target stream top item
messages.summary: Stream entry IDs must be strictly increasing.

messages.value: The ID specified in XADD must be greater than 0-0
messages.summary: 0-0 is reserved as a marker and may not be used as an entry ID.

messages.value: Invalid stream ID specified as stream command argument
messages.summary: The ID must be in `<ms>-<seq>` form, `<ms>`, or one of the auto-generation markers (`*`, `*-N`).

examples.label: auto id
examples.note: ID format is `<ms>-<seq>`; the example value will differ across runs.

returns.summary: Number of entries in the stream.

resp2.summary: Number of entries in the stream at the key, or 0 if the key does not exist.

returns.summary: Stream entries within the specified ID range in ascending ID order, or an error when a range bound cannot be parsed.

oneof.summary: Stream entries within the specified ID range, in ascending ID order.

items.summary: One stream entry: ID followed by its field/value pairs.

prefixItems.name: fields
prefixItems.summary: Flat array of alternating field names and values, in insertion order.

oneof.when: a range bound cannot be parsed

messages.value: Invalid stream ID specified as stream command argument
messages.summary: Range bounds must be `-`, `+`, `<ms>-<seq>`, `<ms>`, or `(<id>` for exclusive bounds.
